title: Palhinhaea riofrioi
taxonomy: Palhinhaea riofrioi is classified as a species of fern within the family Hymenophyllaceae. This family comprises of more than 700 species of filmy ferns, which are known for their delicate and translucent leaves. The genus Palhinhaea is a relatively small one, consisting of only a few species found primarily in South America. Palhinhaea riofrioi is native to Chile and Argentina, where it grows in humid, subtropical environments. It is a relatively rare species, and its classification is recognized by botanists as a distinct and unique member of the Hymenophyllaceae family.

intro: Palhinhaea riofrioi, also known as Riofrio's scale fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds and infections. Bioactive constituents of Palhinhaea riofrioi include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare tea infusions and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
